definition_en: a significant change in the structure or characteristics of a population
meaning_vi: sự thay đổi nhân khẩu học
source_sentence: The country is undergoing a major demographic shift as its population ages rapidly.

definition_en: the average number of children born to a woman over her lifetime
source_sentence: Japan's fertility rate has fallen well below the replacement level.

definition_en: the number of live births per thousand of population per year
source_sentence: The nation's birthrate fell to a record low last year.
collocation_pattern: birthrate fell to a record low

definition_en: the fertility rate at which a population exactly replaces itself from one generation to the next
source_sentence: A fertility rate of 2.1 is generally considered the replacement level.

definition_en: a process by which the proportion of older persons in a population increases
source_sentence: Many developed countries are facing the challenges of population aging.
collocation_pattern: face the challenges of population aging

definition_en: a population in which the proportion of elderly people is rising
source_sentence: An aging population puts pressure on healthcare systems.

definition_en: a population in which a large share of citizens are elderly
source_sentence: Japan is widely regarded as an aged population.

definition_en: people of working age typically in early or mid career
source_sentence: Vietnam currently has a bumper crop of young workers.

definition_en: older members of a population
source_sentence: Larger generations of aging citizens require more public spending.

definition_en: the practice of controlling the number and spacing of one's children
source_sentence: Economic pressures heavily influence family planning decisions.

definition_en: a government regulation limiting families to a maximum of two children
source_sentence: Vietnam has abolished its two-child policy after nearly four decades.

definition_en: government rules designed to influence the number of children people have
meaning_vi: chính sách dân số / sinh sản
source_sentence: The fertility policy was introduced in 1988.

definition_en: a payment given by the government to encourage couples to have children
source_sentence: Officials introduced a baby bonus of around $120.

definition_en: a scheme that offers financial or material incentives to influence behaviour
source_sentence: Some southern provinces have adopted similar reward policies.

definition_en: to officially end a law system or practice
source_sentence: Vietnam has decided to abolish the two-child policy.

definition_en: to completely remove or get rid of something
source_sentence: The country has eliminated its policy of limiting family size.

definition_en: an official rule made by an authority
source_sentence: The new regulation was approved by the National Assembly.

definition_en: a modification to an established course of action by a government or organisation
source_sentence: Many people welcomed the policy change on social media.

definition_en: to give official agreement to a law or rule
source_sentence: The National Assembly approved the new regulation on Tuesday.

definition_en: to make something official or give it formal status
source_sentence: The new law formalizes what had already been common practice.

definition_en: to make people obey a law or rule
source_sentence: The previous policy was only loosely enforced.

definition_en: applied without strict control
source_sentence: Even though the regulation was loosely enforced it remained on the books.

definition_en: a modification of the law
source_sentence: Until the legal change this week party members faced penalties.

definition_en: the elected legislative body of a country
source_sentence: The National Assembly is responsible for passing national laws.

definition_en: members holding positions within the Communist Party
source_sentence: Communist Party officials were the only group strictly bound by the rule.

definition_en: the government department responsible for public health
source_sentence: The Vietnam Ministry of Health announced the policy change online.

definition_en: a poster designed to promote a particular political idea or cause
source_sentence: Officials have rolled out propaganda posters encouraging larger families.

definition_en: an organised effort by the government to inform or influence the public
meaning_vi: chiến dịch tuyên truyền cộng đồng
source_sentence: Public service campaigns rarely change long-term fertility behaviour.

definition_en: the likely state of the economy over many years into the future
meaning_vi: triển vọng kinh tế dài hạn
source_sentence: A falling birthrate threatens the country's long-term economic prospects.

definition_en: the proportional relationship between different age groups in a population
source_sentence: Officials are worried about the country's demographic balance.

definition_en: a period when a population has an unusually favourable age structure for economic growth
source_sentence: Vietnam is currently in a demographic sweet spot.

definition_en: a situation that becomes progressively worse and is hard to reverse
source_sentence: Many Asian countries fear a downward spiral in their populations.

definition_en: to provide the labour spending or resources that keep an economy running
source_sentence: Fewer workers later means fewer people to support the economy.

definition_en: an unusually large number of something used metaphorically
meaning_vi: lực lượng lao động dồi dào
source_sentence: Vietnam has a bumper crop of young workers today.

definition_en: the high level of expense required for everyday life
source_sentence: Many young couples cite the high cost of living in cities as a barrier.
collocation_pattern: high cost of living in cities

definition_en: child care services that ordinary families can pay for
meaning_vi: dịch vụ trông trẻ giá phải chăng
source_sentence: There is a serious lack of affordable child care.

definition_en: the total earnings of a household
source_sentence: Private schooling takes a big chunk of my family income.
collocation_pattern: a big chunk of family income

definition_en: personal choices about whether and when to have children
meaning_vi: quyết định kế hoạch hoá gia đình
source_sentence: The policy change will have little impact on her family planning decisions.
collocation_pattern: have little impact on family planning decisions

definition_en: a school funded by tuition fees rather than the government
source_sentence: Sending a child to a private school is expensive.

definition_en: lessons taken outside the regular school curriculum
source_sentence: She pays for extracurricular classes in English and music.

definition_en: to spend time and money on a person's learning and development
source_sentence: She prefers to invest into her daughter's education and future.

definition_en: to increase the number of children women have on average
source_sentence: The new law is intended to boost a declining fertility rate.

definition_en: to confront a major change in population structure
meaning_vi: đối mặt với sự thay đổi nhân khẩu
source_sentence: Vietnam is facing a demographic shift similar to its neighbours.

definition_en: to restrict something to a maximum number
source_sentence: The previous law limited families to no more than two children.

definition_en: to forfeit additional payments given as rewards
source_sentence: Party members could lose bonuses for having more than two children.

definition_en: to actively persuade people to have children
meaning_vi: khuyến khích các cặp đôi sinh con
source_sentence: Officials are encouraging couples to procreate.

definition_en: to officially launch or introduce something on a large scale
source_sentence: The government has rolled out propaganda posters across the country.

definition_en: to act against an unwanted development
meaning_vi: đối phó với một xu hướng
source_sentence: The measures aim to counter the shrinking size of families.

definition_en: to cause a development to move in the opposite direction
source_sentence: Payments are unlikely to reverse the trend on their own.

definition_en: to convince a person to take a particular action
source_sentence: Public campaigns may not persuade young couples to have more children.

definition_en: to formally choose to follow a particular course of action
source_sentence: Several provinces have adopted similar reward policies.

definition_en: to bring a new rule or system into use for the first time
meaning_vi: ban hành / áp dụng chính sách
source_sentence: The fertility policy was introduced in 1988.

definition_en: to express approval of a new development
meaning_vi: hoan nghênh một sự thay đổi
source_sentence: She welcomed the policy change but doubted its impact.

definition_en: to express strong contempt or rejection of something
source_sentence: Many commenters scorned the idea of large families.

definition_en: to produce a noticeable effect on something
source_sentence: The new law will have little impact on her decisions.

definition_en: to bring up and provide for children until adulthood
source_sentence: She said she could not afford to raise two kids.

definition_en: a fertility rate that is steadily decreasing
source_sentence: The government is alarmed by the country's declining fertility rate.

definition_en: the lowest level ever recorded
source_sentence: The birthrate fell to a record low last year.

definition_en: a year that follows directly after another
source_sentence: It was the third consecutive year of decline.

definition_en: behaviour widely accepted or followed by most people
source_sentence: Having one or two children has long been common practice.

definition_en: households with many children
source_sentence: Many young people no longer aspire to have large families.

definition_en: partners in early adulthood often newly married
source_sentence: Young couples increasingly delay having children.

definition_en: the gradual reduction in family numbers
meaning_vi: quy mô gia đình đang thu hẹp
source_sentence: The shrinking size of families is a growing concern.

definition_en: a public statement published by an authority
source_sentence: The ministry's official post drew thousands of comments.

definition_en: a large portion or share
source_sentence: Tuition takes a big chunk of my family income.

definition_en: as stated or reported by a particular source
source_sentence: According to the United Nations Vietnam is in a period of aging.

definition_en: to give an account of something based on observation or inquiry
source_sentence: The journalist reported from Ho Chi Minh City.

definition_en: to make something officially known to the public
source_sentence: The Ministry of Health announced the policy change on Facebook.

definition_en: to react publicly to something published
source_sentence: Many users responded to the official post with criticism.

definition_en: worries become stronger or more serious
source_sentence: Concerns about the demographic balance have begun to intensify.

definition_en: to be worried that things will get progressively worse
meaning_vi: lo sợ vòng xoáy đi xuống
source_sentence: Vietnam fears a downward spiral similar to Japan's.

definition_en: to put something at risk of harm or failure
source_sentence: Falling birthrates threaten the country's economic prospects.

definition_en: to state firmly that something is the case
source_sentence: She insisted the change would not affect her plans.

definition_en: to do the same thing again in the same way
source_sentence: She did not want to repeat the pattern of her own childhood.

definition_en: evidence indicating something is happening
source_sentence: There is little sign that payments will reverse the trend.

definition_en: in the same way that something happens elsewhere
source_sentence: As is the case in other countries incentives have limited effect.

definition_en: except for
source_sentence: The rule applied to everyone with the exception of party officials.

term: until the legal change this week
definition_en: up to the point when the law was changed recently
meaning_vi: cho đến khi có thay đổi pháp lý tuần này
source_sentence: Penalties remained until the legal change this week.

definition_en: an unlimited number of something
source_sentence: Families can now have as many children as they want.

definition_en: used to introduce a stronger or more accurate statement
source_sentence: In fact officials are now encouraging larger families.

definition_en: for the same well-known reasons people commonly give
meaning_vi: vì tất cả những lý do thường thấy
source_sentence: Commenters opposed large families for all the usual reasons.

definition_en: almost no evidence that something is happening
meaning_vi: có rất ít dấu hiệu rằng
source_sentence: There is little sign that the trend will reverse.

definition_en: to produce only a small effect
source_sentence: The change will have little impact on her decisions.

definition_en: a large portion of something
source_sentence: Schooling takes a big chunk of my income.

definition_en: older members of society who are growing old
source_sentence: Larger generations of aging citizens will require more support.

definition_en: an unusually large amount used figuratively
source_sentence: The country still has a bumper crop of workers.

definition_en: expressing uncertainty about whether something is possible
source_sentence: I am not sure if I can afford another child.

definition_en: rhetorical question expressing reluctance to take on more burden
meaning_vi: tại sao phải có thêm rồi khổ
source_sentence: One commenter wrote why have more and suffer?
